title: Database Concepts
subtitle: Store, Organise, Retrieve — The Power of Data!
overview: Students explore the fundamentals of databases — what they are, why we need them, and how data is stored, organised and retrieved. This session introduces tables, rows, columns, primary keys, and basic SQL queries in a fun and relatable way.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — whiteboard table drawings, student activity sheets, SQL query demos, database diagrams.

gallery.caption: Databases are everywhere — Swiggy, Railways, Schools!

gallery.caption: Students designing their own database tables!

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Diagram showing two related tables
gallery.caption: Tables can be linked — like puzzle pieces fitting together!

keyTopics: What is a Database? — An organised collection of data (like a digital filing cabinet!)
Table — Rows and Columns (like a school attendance register)
Row / Record — One entry (one student's details)
Column / Field — One category of data (Name, Age, Class)
Primary Key — Unique ID for every row (like your Aadhaar number!)
SQL — Structured Query Language (how you talk to a database)
SELECT — Retrieve data from a table
WHERE — Filter results (like a search condition)
INSERT — Add new data into a table
Real-World Databases — Zomato orders, IRCTC tickets, School records

activity.title: Design Your Own School Database
steps: Think: What information does your school need to store about students?
Draw a table on paper — add columns: StudentID, Name, Class, Age, City
Fill in 5 rows with made-up student data
Identify the Primary Key — which column is unique for every student?
Open SQLiteOnline.com and create your table using SQL
Write a SELECT query to retrieve all students
Use WHERE to find only students from a specific class
Challenge: Add a new student using INSERT INTO!

options: An organised collection of data stored and accessed electronically
quiz.explanation: A database is an organised collection of data! Think of it like a super-smart digital filing cabinet that stores, organises and lets you quickly find any information.

quiz.q: In a database table, what is a ROW also called?
quiz.explanation: A Row = a Record! Each row in a table stores one complete entry — like one student's full details (name, age, class, city).

quiz.q: In a database table, what is a COLUMN also called?
quiz.explanation: A Column = a Field! Each column stores one type of information — like Name, Age, or City — for all records in the table.

options: The first column in any table
A unique identifier for every row in a table
The most important piece of data
A password to access the database
quiz.explanation: Primary Key = unique ID for every row! Like your Aadhaar number or Roll Number — no two rows can have the same primary key value.
SQL = Structured Query Language! It's the language you use to talk to a database — to ask questions, add data, update or delete records.

quiz.q: Which SQL command is used to RETRIEVE data from a table?
quiz.explanation: SELECT is how you ask the database to show you data! Example: SELECT * FROM Students; — this fetches all records from the Students table.

quiz.q: What does the WHERE clause do in a SQL query?
options: Filters results based on a condition
quiz.explanation: WHERE filters your results! Example: SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Class = '7'; — this only returns students in Class 7.

quiz.q: Which SQL command adds a new record into a table?
quiz.explanation: INSERT INTO adds new data! Example: INSERT INTO Students (Name, Age) VALUES ('Riya', 12); — adds a new student named Riya.

quiz.q: A school attendance register is a real-life example of a:
quiz.explanation: An attendance register is just like a database table! Each row is one student (record), each column is one detail (field) like Name, Date, Present/Absent.

quiz.q: Which of these is a real-world example of a database being used?
options: Booking a train ticket on IRCTC
quiz.explanation: IRCTC uses a massive database! When you book a ticket, it stores your name, train number, seat, date — all as records in a database table.

quiz.q: What is the correct SQL syntax to select all columns from a table called 'Students'?
quiz.explanation: SELECT * FROM Students; — the * means 'all columns'. This is one of the most used SQL queries you'll write!

quiz.q: Can two rows in a table have the same PRIMARY KEY value?
options: Only if the table is large
No, primary keys must be unique
Yes, if the names are different
quiz.explanation: No! Primary keys must always be unique. That's the whole point — it's like a roll number, no two students can share the same one.

quiz.q: Which of these best describes a DATABASE TABLE?
options: A photo stored on a computer
A collection of data arranged in rows and columns
quiz.explanation: A table stores data in rows and columns — just like a spreadsheet! Each row is one record and each column is one field of information.

quiz.q: In a Students table, which column would make the BEST primary key?
quiz.explanation: StudentID is the best primary key! Names can repeat (two students named Riya), but a StudentID is always unique for every student.

quiz.q: Zomato stores your order history, address, and payment details. This is an example of:
quiz.explanation: Zomato uses databases to store millions of orders, user profiles, restaurant menus and delivery details — all organised in tables and retrieved using SQL!
